incorrects.description: Student selected 4 — the value for Fish instead of the difference

params.description: Visual comparison showing Dogs (7) and Fish (4) with difference highlighted

steps.text: You counted the fish. The question asks how many fewer fish than dogs—that means find the difference between the two numbers.

incorrects.description: Student selected 7 — the value for Dogs instead of the difference

params.description: Visual comparison showing Dogs (7) and Fish (4) with difference highlighted

steps.text: You counted the dogs. How many fewer means compare—subtract the smaller number from the larger number to find the difference.

incorrects.description: Student selected 11 — added 7 and 4 instead of subtracting

params.description: Visual comparison showing Dogs (7) and Fish (4) with difference highlighted

steps.text: Here's a hint: how many fewer means find the difference. That means subtract, not add. Take the smaller number away from the larger number.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly — system models the correct answer

params.description: System demonstrates subtracting 4 from 7 to show difference of 3

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. Dogs have 7 symbols and Fish have 4 symbols. How many fewer means compare or find the difference—so we subtract: 7 minus 4 equals 3. The answer is 3.
